model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed-step budget (256), increasing batch size raises unique examples seen per run, while switching to AdamW with a modestly higher LR typically improves early optimization for flat CIFAR-10 MLPs without adding code complexity.","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The previous step regressed validation loss, so disabling the scheduler restores the better constant-LR behavior while keeping code simple and safe.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With only 256 optimization steps, reducing decoupled weight decay can improve early fit and lower validation loss before over-regularization dominates.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"A modest width increase should improve representation quality for the flat MLP while remaining simple and stable within the same fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After width scaling helped, adding one more hidden block is a targeted capacity increase that can improve decision boundaries without changing training logic.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After increasing model capacity, a slightly lower AdamW learning rate can reduce optimization noise and improve validation loss at the same step budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The LR reduction regressed loss, so reverting to 1e-3 restores the better-performing setting for the current architecture.","secondary_modes":["layout","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"In fixed-step mode, increasing batch size doubles examples seen during training, which can improve validation loss for this stable AdamW+BatchNorm MLP setup.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strong gain from larger batches suggests this workload benefits from maximizing examples per fixed step; moving to 512 tests that trend directly with a single simple change.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With very large-batch training now working well, a higher AdamW learning rate is a strong next probe to improve optimization progress per fixed step.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher LR caused a large regression, so reverting to 1e-3 restores the best-known optimization setting for batch size 512.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Performance improved dramatically as batch size increased, so testing 1024 directly probes whether seeing even more examples per fixed step further reduces validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last three batch-size increases each improved validation loss, so pushing to 2048 is the most direct continuation of the strongest observed trend under fixed-step evaluation.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"At batch size 2048, optimization likely benefits from partial LR scaling; 1.5e-3 is a conservative increase to improve progress without repeating the instability seen at 2e-3 with smaller batches.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The move from 1e-3 to 1.5e-3 improved loss at batch size 2048, so a smaller follow-up increase to 1.8e-3 tests for additional gain while limiting instability risk.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With very large-batch AdamW, a lower second beta can improve responsiveness of adaptive updates and sometimes yields better fixed-step convergence.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.98)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With large-batch training, lowering beta1 can make updates more reactive and may improve fixed-step convergence beyond the current AdamW setting.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.98)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Lowering beta1 caused a clear regression, so reverting to the prior beta setting restores the best known optimizer dynamics.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.9e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Final-step exploitation: a small LR increase from the current best-performing regime may yield additional convergence without materially changing stability.","secondary_modes":["indexing","micro"]}
